model.name: multicast
model.title: Multicast
model.description: Routes the same message to multiple paths either sequentially or in parallel.
model.label: eip,routing

id.description: Sets the id of this node

note.description: Sets the note of this node

description.description: Sets the description of this node

disabled.label: advanced
disabled.description: Whether to disable this EIP from the route during build time. Once an EIP has been disabled then it cannot be enabled later at runtime.

aggregationStrategy.description: Reference to the AggregationStrategy to assemble the replies from the multicasts into a single outgoing message. By default Camel uses the last reply as the outgoing message.

aggregationStrategyMethodName.label: advanced
aggregationStrategyMethodName.description: The method name to use when using a POJO as the AggregationStrategy.

aggregationStrategyMethodAllowNull.label: advanced
aggregationStrategyMethodAllowNull.description: If true then null is used as the oldExchange when there is no data to aggregate, when using POJOs as the AggregationStrategy.

parallelAggregate.label: advanced
parallelAggregate.description: If enabled then the aggregate method on AggregationStrategy can be called concurrently. Notice that this would require the implementation of AggregationStrategy to be implemented as thread-safe. By default this is false meaning that Camel synchronizes the call to the aggregate method. Though in some use-cases this can be used to archive higher performance when the AggregationStrategy is implemented as thread-safe.

parallelProcessing.description: If enabled then sending messages to the multicasts occurs concurrently. The caller thread still waits until all messages are fully processed before it continues.

synchronous.description: When enabled then the same thread is used to continue routing after the multicast is complete, even if parallel processing is enabled.

streaming.description: If enabled then Camel will process replies out-of-order, in the order they come back. If disabled, Camel will process replies in the same order as defined by the multicast.

stopOnException.label: advanced
stopOnException.description: If enabled then stops further multicast processing if an exception or failure occurred during processing of an exchange, and the caused exception will be thrown. The default behavior is to not stop but continue processing till the end.

timeout.label: advanced
timeout.description: Total timeout in millis when using parallel processing. If the multicast has not been able to process all replies within the given timeframe, then the timeout triggers and the multicast breaks out and continues.

executorService.label: advanced
executorService.description: Reference to a custom thread pool to use for parallel processing. Setting this option implies parallel processing.

onPrepare.label: advanced
onPrepare.description: Reference to a processor for preparing the exchange to be sent. Can be used to deep-clone messages that should be sent.

shareUnitOfWork.label: advanced
shareUnitOfWork.description: Shares the unit of work with the parent and each of the multicast exchanges. By default each multicast exchange has its own individual unit of work.

CamelMulticastIndex.label: producer
CamelMulticastIndex.description: An index counter that increases for each Exchange being multicasted. The counter starts from 0.

CamelMulticastComplete.label: producer
CamelMulticastComplete.description: Whether this Exchange is the last.

CamelToEndpoint.label: producer
CamelToEndpoint.description: Endpoint URI where this Exchange is being sent to
